title: Chapter 5 - HTTP
description: Check if you understand basic HTTP ideas: methods, status codes, and bodies.

questions.prompt: Which HTTP method is most commonly used to **read** a page or fetch data?

explanations.overview: GET is used to request data from a server.

byOptionId.d: Correct. GET is for fetching/reading data.
byOptionId.c: POST usually creates something or sends data to the server.
byOptionId.a: DELETE is for removing a resource.
byOptionId.b: That’s not a real HTTP method.

questions.prompt: What does an HTTP status code `404` usually mean?

options.label: The server is asking you to reboot
The page or resource was not found

explanations.overview: 404 means the server can’t find what you asked for at that path.

byOptionId.b: That would be a 2xx success code like 200.
byOptionId.c: Correct. The resource doesn’t exist at that location (or the server can’t find it).
byOptionId.d: Encryption is about HTTPS/TLS, not status codes.

options.label: Headers are the main content, and the body is just metadata.
Headers are metadata, and the body can contain HTML or JSON.
HTTP has no headers at all.

explanations.overview: Headers describe the message, and the body carries the main content.

byOptionId.a: That’s flipped: headers are metadata, body is the main content.
byOptionId.b: Correct. Body content can be HTML, JSON, files, etc.
byOptionId.d: Any program can use HTTP (apps, scripts, servers).

questions.prompt: What does an HTTP 5xx status code generally indicate?

options.label: The client asked for an encrypted connection

explanations.overview: 5xx codes mean the server failed to fulfill a valid request.

byOptionId.a: Encryption is about TLS; 5xx are server failures.
byOptionId.d: Plugins are not indicated by 5xx codes.
